address.addrLine: Department of Near and Middle Eastern Collections

p.foreign: در مقاله شخصی سیاح که در تفصیل قضیه باب نوشته است

summary.p:
Besides the full account of this MS. on pp. 663-665 of my Catalogue and
Description of 27 Bábí Manuscripts, its exact appearance can be judged from the

photo-lithographic facsimile forming vol. 1 of my Traveller's Narrative, written to
illustrate the Episode of the Bab, published by the Cambridge University Press int
1891, while the Introduction in vol. 11, containing the English translation, gives
full particulars concerning the work, of which, therefore, no further account need be
given here.

In the fuller 1892 catalogue entry, Browne sets out, for the first time in print, his argument that the anonymous author is Bahá'u'lláh's eldest son, 'Abbás Efendi (later known as 'Abdu'l-Bahá, called by the Bábís Áqá-yi- or Ḥaḍrat-i-Sirru'lláh).

msItem.title: Maqāla-ʼi shakhṣī-i sayyāḥ kih dar tafṣīl-i qaz̤īyah-ʾi Bāb nivishtah ast
مقالۀ شخصی سياح كه در تفصبل قضيۀ باب نوشته است
A Traveller's Narrative, Written to Illustrate the Episode of the Báb
msItem.note: Place and date of transcription not stated by Browne. Browne notes that "the facsimile forming vol. i. of my Traveller's Narrative... accurately represents the original," i.e. this manuscript was reproduced in facsimile in his 1891 edition.
msItem.bibl: See sub classmark BBA. 1 in Browne's "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bābī Manuscripts (Continuation)", in Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland (Oct., 1892): 637-710.

Browne used this manuscript as the basis for the facsimile text volume (vol. i) of his own two-volume edition and translation of the work, published by Cambridge University Press in February 1892; volume ii contains his introduction, translation, and notes.

handDesc.handNote: good naskh. Title on f. 1a written in red, with "an irregular system of punctuation with red dots" throughout.

history.provenance: Given or sent to E. G. Browne by Bahá'u'lláh's sons from Acre.
history.acquisition: Passed to Cambridge University Library in 1936 (the "Browne Bequest").

recordHist.source: Summary, physical description and provenance copied from R. A. Nicholson: A descriptive catalogue of the Oriental MSS belonging to the late E.G. Browne. Cambridge, 1932; further expanded from E. G. Browne, "Catalogue and Description of 27 Bábī Manuscripts (Continued)," Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society of Great Britain and Ireland, Oct. 1892, pp. 663-666.

availability.p: Entry to read in the Library is permitted only on presentation of a valid reader's card for admissions procedures contact Cambridge University Library Admissions). Contact near_eastern@lib.cam.ac.uk for further information on the availability of this manuscript

revisionDesc.change: Draft checked and updated with additional encoding and authority control by Yasmin Faghihi.

Syed Gillani Claude (merge) Merged content from the corresponding Browne press-mark draft record (babi_ms/original_b_nos/BBA_1.xml): expanded the summary, provenance (the Acre origin, the Bombay-lithograph corroborating parcel, and Browne's argument for 'Abdu'l-Bahá's authorship), and hand note. Flagged rather than silently resolved a substantial physical-description conflict: this record's Nicholson-derived physDesc gives 78 folios at 25 x 20 cm, while Browne's own 1892 account gives 108 folios at 18 x 10.6 cm - a cataloguer should check the manuscript directly. Person authority key reused from elsewhere in the collection where already established (E. G. Browne); left blank for 'Abbás Efendi, Badí'u'lláh, Diyá'u'lláh, and Mírzá Abu'l-Fażl, none of which have existing Fihrist/VIAF keys in this collection. Not yet reviewed by a cataloguer for this addition.
Values generated from legacy catalogue using a combination of AI and manual editing.
Julian Cook Assigned role(s) to every person having an id (Fihrist data cleanup project).
Andrew Morrison Updated key attributes using update-keys.xsl
Andrew Morrison Added summary using add-summaries.xsl
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/bodleian/fihrist-mss/tree/master/processing/convertFihrist2Bodley.xsl
